title: podman load interprets tags as repository
body:
The below example exports an image with tag `ttt` and imports it back with `podman load`.  Then the `ttt` appears in the repository column of `podman images`.

```
$ echo "FROM scratch" > Containerfile
$ buildah bud --tag rrr:ttt
STEP 1/1: FROM scratch
COMMIT rrr:ttt
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 5f70bf18a086 done  
Copying config 7a1697d7e1 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 7a1697d7e18
Successfully tagged localhost/rrr:ttt
7a1697d7e1812f4d6759f5722c76fbea49b9d90b31f2aa811ffc093aa5ab50a1

$ podman images
REPOSITORY     TAG         IMAGE ID      CREATED         SIZE
localhost/rrr  ttt         7a1697d7e181  26 seconds ago  2.1 kB
## so here rrr is under repository, and ttt is under tag

## according to https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/docs/containers-transports.5.md
## the :ttt at the end of the next line means a tag, not a repository
$ skopeo copy containers-storage:localhost/rrr:ttt oci-archive:sss.tar:ttt
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 5f70bf18a086 done  
Copying config 7a1697d7e1 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures

$ podman rmi localhost/rrr:ttt
Untagged: localhost/rrr:ttt
Deleted: 7a1697d7e1812f4d6759f5722c76fbea49b9d90b31f2aa811ffc093aa5ab50a1

$ podman load < sss.tar
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 4ca545ee6d5d done  
Copying config 7a1697d7e1 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Loaded image(s): localhost/ttt:latest

$ podman images
REPOSITORY     TAG         IMAGE ID      CREATED        SIZE
localhost/ttt  latest      7a1697d7e181  3 minutes ago  2.11 kB
```
I expect on the previous line to see `ttt` in the TAG column.

For the record, sss.tar:index.json is:
> {"schemaVersion":2,"manifests":[{"mediaType":"application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json","digest":"sha256:57fd9fb21c89258a4947cb12504e0a2c56aacc89792db0115604a4dba2e4cad4","size":343,"annotations":{"org.opencontainers.image.ref.name":"ttt"}}]}

So it contains `"annotations":{"org.opencontainers.image.ref.name":"ttt"}`.

comments.body: Note: the original SIZE was 2.1kb, the final SIZE is 2.11kb.

The `org.opencontainers.image.ref.name` annotation ( https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/blob/main/annotations.md ) does not have a defined repository/tag components; so arguing whether it is a repository or a tag is somewhat a lost cause because it could easily be not valid as either (e.g. `a@a` is a valid value of the annotation). AFAICS Podman is trying to parse it as a full reference name, i.e. `repo[{:tag|@digest}]`, which allows representing the traditional container repo references of all kinds in the annotation.

It’s a fair point that the containers-transports(5) man page should talk about something like references and not tags; as far as the actual behavior goes, it seems to me that Podman is doing the best it can in this situation.

> The `org.opencontainers.image.ref.name` annotation ( https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/blob/main/annotations.md ) does not have a defined repository/tag components; so arguing whether it is a repository or a tag is somewhat a lost cause because it could easily be not valid as either (e.g. `a@a` is a valid value of the annotation). AFAICS Podman is trying to parse it as a full reference name, i.e. `repo[{:tag|@digest}]`, which allows representing the traditional container repo references of all kinds in the annotation.
> 
> It’s a fair point that the containers-transports(5) man page should talk about something like references and not tags; as far as the actual behavior goes, it seems to me that Podman is doing the best it can in this situation.

Fixed in https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1376 .
Any idea why the SIZE column has changed 2.1 → 2.11kb?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
No idea on size change other then perhaps it was rounding, and now giving you more precision, Since man page was fixed, closing.
